human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for hospitality-and-travel guest-experience specificity, healthcare-and-care-delivery boundary discipline, source quality, PHI handling, authorization/referral/eligibility prerequisites, clinical and payer escalation, functionality coverage, and validated spec-pack completeness.

review_notes: All required domain scenario families are covered: missing authorization/referral/eligibility dependency, PHI-sensitive controlled handling, and clinical or payer-interpretation escalation.
All queue-level must-pass families are covered: policy exception request, missing detail for safe resolution, and communication handoff to another lane.
All required functional abilities for shared baseline, service-support-and-resolution, and healthcare-and-care-delivery are represented and validated.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary controls restrict the lane to hospitality-and-travel guest-experience service resolution with healthcare-administrative guardrails for patient/member, payer, referral, authorization, PHI, clinical-risk, and claim-administrative contexts.

Authority sources cover public healthcare regulator guidance, public payer operating guidance, recognized healthcare experience measure guidance, DOJ lodging accessibility, DOT passenger disability handling, FTC lodging fee disclosure, and PCI contact-center payment-security handling.

Scenario set covers normal in-scope execution, missing facts, policy exceptions, eligibility/authorization/referral blockers, PHI handling, clinical and payer escalation, unsupported requests, handoffs, repeat failures, terminology, conflicting policy, and tenant adaptation.
Runtime remains specification-based with explicit promotion requirements for any later retained-implementation transition.
notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains; tenant, payer, provider, property, supplier, state, jurisdiction, processor, and brand differences are handled through runtime retrieval and escalation rules.
This package is ready for validated operations under specification-based execution controls.
